item.title: 可观察性 | 简化的 | Observability | Simplified
item.description:
可观察性是我们开发人员圈子中的一个常用术语； 通常与监控和警报相结合。 许多流行的工具声称可以端到端地解决您的问题，并且围绕开源技术和协议进行了大量交流。 本文试图简化其中一些术语以及可观察性的真正工作原理。

什么是可观察性？

可观察性是拥有有关系统的数据的实践，可以帮助您了解未知事物。 它不涉及您的指标仪表板（即监控）或您设置的警报。 检测和收集数据的过程使您能够观察软件系统的行为方式，了解它们的健康状况并收集有关它们如何工作的详细知识，这就是可观察性。

有 3 种常见类型的数据源（称为遥测数据）可帮助您揭开真相：

如果您是开发人员，那么在测试代码时添加的第一件事就是日志。 它们可以是系统生成的（例如由 nginx）或手动生成的，并且可以包含各种有助于了解有关代码执行的重要信息的数据。

它们是量化软件的某个行为方面的数值，这些数值保存在时间序列存储中以供在一段时间内查看。 大多数软件都会发出指标，无论是在 pod 上运行的服务还是 k8s 集群本身。

换句话说，吞吐量（每分钟请求数）或每分钟 API 调用的平均响应时间是您熟悉的一些指标，并且必须在仪表板中注意到这些指标。

您可以将跟踪视为一种特殊形式的日志，旨在提供有关您的“请求”所采取的步骤集的详细信息。 它将您的整个执行分成更小的块，包括代码级逻辑、数据库查询和下游调用。 这些执行（称为“跨度”）很容易通过它们的名称和前缀来识别。 如果您的团队已经设置了跟踪，您可能会看到的常用名称：

  1. 数据存储 - 数据库查询和连接处理步骤

  2. 外部 - 通过网络在您的服务外部进行的调用
    函数 - 当前程序中的代码执行 根据您的情况，可以出现其他跨度名称

    仪器代理。

什么是监控？

监控是您使用遥测数据设置仪表板和指标可视化的部分，您已经知道需要跟踪这些指标以在任何时间点查看系统的运行状况。 可观察性意味着拥有这样的数据，即使您不知道需要跟踪什么，您也可以足够深入地调查您的系统。

这是如何运作的？

下面是一个示例流程，展示了可观察性在集成到您的微服务架构中时的样子。

通常，它涉及向现有代码添加一小段代码/程序（检测代理）。

好的，但我需要知道它是如何工作的吗？ 不，不是真的。 您可以决定继续使用商业工具，您需要做的就是按照几行说明进行设置。 上面提到的所有步骤都由他们处理，因此细节被抽象出来，您可以直接开始监控您的系统。

警告：随着系统规模的扩大，商业工具的成本将开始下降，您可能会考虑转向 OSS。

奖金部分：

在过去的几年中，术语 o11y 开始因可观察性一词而流行起来（例如 The event o11yfest ）。

现在你说怎么办？ 查找此代码的输出以了解操作方法：

 def word_encoder(word): word = word.replace(" ","") #removing spaces mid_char_count = len(word) - 2 encoding = word[0].lower() + str(mid_char_count) + word[-1].lower() print(encoding) return encoding word_encoder("observability")

您是否在想“这种灵感来自哪里？” 尝试找到这些函数调用的输出，您就会知道答案:)

 word_encoder("kubernetes") word_encoder("Andreessen Horowitz")

有趣的事实：这些词是“ numeronym ”

如果您遇到任何其他需要简化的行话，请在评论中提及！

 We are shortly publishing a comparison of the most relevant open source & commercial tools for observability. If you would like to get a copy of it, sign up below!

一旦暂停，drdroid 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，drdroid 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，drdroid 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 drdroid 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 drdroid 的方法：

降低 drdroid 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 drdroid 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Observability is a common term thrown around in our developer circles; often coupled with monitoring & alerting. A lot of popular tools claim to be solving your problems end-to-end and a lot of exchanges go on around open source technologies and protocols around this. This article tries to simplify some of these terms and how observability really works.

What is Observability?

Observability is the practice of having data about your system that can help you know the unknown. It doesn’t refer to your metrics dashboards (that is monitoring) or to the alerts you set up. The process of instrumenting and collecting data that enables you to observe how your software systems behave, be aware of their health and gather detailed knowledge of how they are working is observability.

There are 3 common types of data sources (called telemetry data) that help you uncover the truth:

1. Logs

If you’re a developer, the first thing you add while testing your code is logs. They can be either system generated (e.g. by nginx) or manually generate and can have a variety of data that helps in knowing vital information about the execution of the code.

2. Metrics

They are numerical values quantifying a certain behavioural aspect of your software, which is saved in a time series storage for seeing over a period of time. Most software emit metrics, be it your service running on a pod or the k8s cluster itself.

To put it into context, the throughput (Requests per minute) or Avg response time of your API calls per minute are some metrics that you'll be familiar with and must have noticed in dashboards.

3. Traces

You can think of traces as a specialized form of logs, designed to give details around the set of steps your “request” took. It splits your entire execution into smaller chunks, including code level logic, DB queries & downstream calls. These executions (called “spans”) are easily identifiable with their names and their prefixes. Common names that you might have seen if your team has already setup traces:

  1. Datastore - DB queries and connection handling steps

  2. External - Calls made outside your service over a network
    protocol like HTTP, MQTT etc.

  3. Function - Code execution within the current program There are other span names that can come up based on your

    instrumentation agent.

What is monitoring?

Monitoring is the part where you use the telemetry data to set up dashboards and visualisations of metrics you already know that you need to track to view the system's health at any point in time. Observability means having data such that even when you don’t know what you need to track, you can investigate your system deeply enough.

How does this work?

Below is a sample flow of how observability, when integrated within your micro-services architecture, looks like.

Instrumentation: Refers to how the telemetry data is generated within the system. Typically, it involves adding a small piece of code/program (instrumentation agent) to your existing code.

Ok, but do I need to know how this works? 😬

No. Not really. You can decide to go ahead with a commercial tool and all you need to do is follow a couple of lines of instructions to set it up. All the steps mentioned above are taken care of by them so the details are abstracted out and you can directly start monitoring your system.

Caveat: As your system scales, the cost of the commercial tools will start pinching and you might consider moving to OSS.

Bonus section:

Over the last couple of years, the term o11y is starting to get popular for the word Observability (e.g. The event o11yfest).

Now you say how? Find the output of this code to know how:

def word_encoder(word):
    word = word.replace(" ","") #removing spaces
    mid_char_count = len(word) - 2
    encoding = word[0].lower() + str(mid_char_count) + word[-1].lower()
    print(encoding)
    return encoding

word_encoder("observability")

Are you thinking “where is this inspiration coming from?” Try to find the output for these function calls and you'll know the answer :)

word_encoder("kubernetes")
word_encoder("Andreessen Horowitz")

Fun fact: These words are “numeronym”

If you have come across any other jargon that needs to be simplified, mention them in the comments!

We are shortly publishing a comparison of the most relevant open source & commercial tools for observability. If you would like to get a copy of it, sign up below!

Once suspended, drdroid will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, drdroid will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by drdroid will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If drdroid is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag drdroid:

Make all posts by drdroid less visible

drdroid consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging drdroid will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: dimittal

item.title: 自然语言处理应用 | Natural Language Processing Applications
item.description:
软件开发中心

发表于 4 月 21 日

自然语言是人类交流的主要方法。 它由文字组成，并通过文本、语音和手势来表达。 手势不是这篇文章的主角，所以我们将继续进行文字和演讲。 在我们的数字化世界中，我们非常鼓励与设备进行交流，顺便说一句，阅读这篇文章确实是一种文本交流。 所有这些因素导致了一个事实，即 NLP 已经成为 ML / DS 主题的前沿。 那么我们对 NLP 了解多少？

什么是自然语言处理？

自然语言处理 (NLP) 是人工智能的一个子领域，旨在让计算机像人类一样理解文本和口语。 这个领域对于企业处理来自电子邮件、社交媒体、聊天、调查等的大量数据并从中获取见解非常有价值。NLP 是一种已经在我的许多企业中使用的技术，我们将涵盖其中的一些在下面的部分中。

情绪分析


我们说话的方式常常伴随着使单词的字面意思过于复杂的短语、情感或讽刺。 这些因素在社交媒体中经常遇到，计算机很难理解。 这就是 NLP 主要是其子集 NLU（自然语言理解）的用武之地。 借助 NLU，计算机可以区分情绪并识别文本语气。 情感分析是一种用于识别数据的积极性、消极性或中性的 NLP 方法。 企业经常对文本数据进行情绪分析，以跟踪客户评论中对其品牌和产品的看法，并更好地了解其目标市场。

有几种方法可以将 NLP 用于情感分析任务。 这些都是：

  • 基于规则——使用一些 NLP 技术，例如词干提取、标记化、词性标记。 工作原理如下：定义每组词（负/正），统计每个词组在句子中出现的次数。 根据每个情感组的计数，判断句子是正面的还是负面的。 如果甚至返回中性。 但是基于规则的方法可能会达到一个非常复杂的系统。
  • 自动——这是机器学习实现的地方。 情感分析通常是一个分类问题。 我们已经标记了数据，并试图在给定带有评论/评论/反馈等的句子数据的情况下找到合适的情绪。这种方法的关键方面是合适的。 特征提取和准备特征向量。 为此，我们使用 Bag-of-words、Word Embeddings、TF-IDF。
  • 混合——上述技术的组合。 通常会产生更准确的结果。

请继续关注航空公司情绪分析的实施。

语言翻译


每个人都需要非常快速地翻译一些东西。 无论是谷歌翻译还是任何其他类似的应用程序。 此类应用程序基于称为机器翻译的技术。 它被定义为在不丢失含义的情况下自动将文本从一种语言转换为另一种语言的过程。 与基于规则解决问题的字典之上构建翻译应用程序的时代相比，当前最先进的机器翻译方法 (NMT) 使用神经网络来预测单词的可能性. 与 SMT（统计机器翻译）和基于规则的相比，NMT 的最大优势是速度快，能够根据需要学习单词并重用。

自动校正和预测


今天，有几种可用的程序可以通过检查我们输入的文本的语法和拼写来帮助我们避免电子邮件、消息和其他文档中的拼写和语法错误（例如 Grammarly）。 这些程序包括范围广泛的功能，包括建议同义词、修正语法和拼写错误、为清楚起见而重新措辞，甚至估计用户陈述的隐含语气的能力。 另一个称为“自动预测”的 NLP 功能建议对我们已经开始输入的文本进行自动文本预测。 这有助于用户节省时间并简化他们的工作。 这些是构建此类模型的步骤：

  • 识别拼写错误的单词。
  • 搜索与错误单词相距 N 编辑距离的字符串。
  • 过滤环。
  • 基于单词概率的顺序。
  • 选择最好的情况。 文本预测基于它出现的可能性使用 n-gram。 出现在文档中的连续的单词、标记或其他符号组称为 N-gram。

定向广告


如果您曾经在任何在线商店中搜索任何产品或对象，您会经常在其他网站上看到此类物品和其他相关产品的广告。 这种在线定向广告称为定向广告，是借助自然语言处理进行的。

定向广告的重要组成部分是关键词匹配。 只有那些搜索与广告所附术语相似的术语的人才会看到链接到特定单词或短语的广告。 这显然是不够的； 其他因素，例如他们随后访问的网站和他们看起来感兴趣的网页，也被考虑在内，为他们提供他们可能感兴趣的产品的大量广告。

由于广告只展示给真正对产品感兴趣的客户，这是根据他们的在线行为评估的，因此许多企业从中受益匪浅，并节省了大量资金。

聊天机器人


我个人认为聊天机器人是如今提供在线服务的大多数企业、政府网站不可或缺的一部分。 它们无处不在。 那么，AI 驱动的机器人与基于规则的机器人有何不同？

我们将大多数常见机器人称为“基于规则”的机器人。 他们被要求认真遵守作者制定的对话准则。 当用户输入特定命令时，基于规则的机器人将自动提供预先写好的答案。 然而，如果不遵循这些准则，传统的机器人可能难以为用户提供有价值的信息。 缺乏作为人类对话重要组成部分的适应性。 这些是让 AI 聊天机器人如此特别和有价值的主要因素：

  • 理解自然语言。
  • 沟通的方式不仅仅是问答。
  • 连续的提高。 要训​​练聊天机器人理解自然语言，您需要大量数据。 可以使用包括社交媒体、论坛和客户服务记录在内的众多来源来获取此信息。 为了适应多种情况，数据必须多样化和标记。

作为下一步，您必须预处理收集的数据。 将文本标记为更小的位、清理和规范化数据是执行的关键步骤。

  • 文本规范化——保持数据的一致性，将文本转换为小写，删除标点符号等。
  • 标记化——将文本分解成更小的部分，例如单词或短语。
  • 从文本中删除停用词——如“the”、“is”和“and”等不会显着增加数据集意义的术语。
  • 词形还原——降低数据的维度。
  • 词性标注——识别文本中每个词的语法作用，例如名词、动词或形容词。 那么了解哪种 NLP 技术适合您的业务需求至关重要。 它可以是情感分析、基于机器学习的方法、关键字匹配系统或语言模型。

这篇博文不应该在结束时不提及趋势 ChatGPT。 由 OpenAI 开发的最先进的自然语言处理 (NLP) 模型——ChatGPT 是著名的 GPT-3 (Generative Pertained Transformer 3) 模型的变体，该模型在大量文本数据上进行训练 ( ~ 570GB 的数据集，包括网页、书籍和其他来源）以产生类似人类的回复。 它是机器学习自然语言处理模型的大型语言模型 (LLM) 类的扩展。 随着过去几年计算机能力的提高，这些模型得到了扩展。 随着输入数据集和参数空间变大，LLM 变得更加强大。 语言模型分析数据中的文本以计算后续单词的可能性。 几种概率方法用于训练语言模型。 可训练参数的数量因同一系列模型的成员而异。 相当强大的工具，目前有足够的限制。 期待调查和测试 GPT4……

情感分析——示例代码

让我们尝试对航空公司数据集执行情感分析。

import nltk from nltk.corpus import stopwords from nltk.corpus import wordnet as wn from nltk.stem.wordnet import WordNetLemmatizer

从 nltk.tokenize 导入 word_tokenize


上面我们可以看到主要导入的 Natural Language Toolkit——NLP 和停用词库的集合——不为句子提供任何意义的词集合，因此应该从数据集中删除。

dataset = pd.read_csv("../input/airline/airline_sentiment_analysis.csv")


dataset.info()


检查我们的数据集中没有任何空值。

colors=sns.color_palette() pd.Series(dataset['airline_sentiment']).value_counts().plot(kind='pie',colors=colors,labels=['Negative','Postive'],explode=[0.05,0.05],shadow=True,autopct='%.2f',fontsize=12,figsize=(6,6)) plt.show()


很明显，我们对负面推文的数据不平衡。 因此，我们必须使用处理不平衡数据的技术。 数据集['clean_text']=数据集['text'].apply(lambda x: cleaning_text(x))`
词形还原——消除所有后缀，只留下词根。

词形还原器 = WordNetLemmatizer()

上面的代码片段只是快速简单地清理文本，从文本列中删除停用词、数字和特殊字符。

def convert_Sentiment(sentiment): if sentiment == "positive": return 1 elif sentiment == "negative": return 0

现在我们必须对清理后的文本数据集执行相同的操作。 我们将使用 TF-IDF 向量化器将单词转换为向量，不仅要考虑单词出现的次数，还要考虑该单词的重要性。

tfid = TfidfVectorizer()

现在回到不平衡数据问题，因为我们将其悬而未决。

smote = SMOTE() x_sm,y_sm = smote.fit_resample(X_vect,y)


现在我们已经完成了数据集的准备，我们可以进入训练模型和测试结果的最后步骤。

精度 = accuracy_score(pred,y_test)

打印（'准确度：'+ str（准确度））打印（分类报告（pred，y_test））cm = confusion_matrix（pred，y_test）

监控客户反馈并从中获取情绪有助于企业提高产品和服务质量。 因此，导致更高的销售额。 这个模型会有更多的价值，因为我们可以获得更长时间范围的数据。

我们创造有意义的产品，坚持客户的商业目标。 主要发展领域：数字健康、会计、教育。

原文:

Software Development Hub

Posted on Apr 21

The Natural Language is a principal method of human communication. It comprises of words and is expressed by means of text, speech and gestures. The gesture is not the main character of this post so were going to proceed with text and speech. In our digitalized world we are highly encouraged to communicate with devices and by the way reading this post is indeed a communication by text. All these factors have led to a fact that NLP has become in the front lines of ML / DS topics. So what do we know about NLP ?

What is NLP?

Natural Language Processing (NLP) is a subfield of AI that deals with making computers to understand text and spoken words just like we humans do. This field is highly valuable for businesses to process and get insights from huge volume of data coming from emails, social media, chats, surveys etc. NLP is a technology that is already in use my many businesses and we are going to cover some of them in the below sections.

Sentiment Analysis


The way we talk is often accompanied with the phrases, sentiments or sarcasm that overcomplicate the literal meaning of words. These factors are frequently faced in Social Media and are quite difficult for computers to understand. This is where NLP mainly its subset NLU (Natural Language Understanding) comes in place. With NLU computers can differentiate among the sentiments and identify a tone of text. Sentiment analysis is a NLP method for identifying the positivity, negativity, or neutrality of data. Businesses frequently do sentiment analysis on textual data to track the perception of their brands and products in customer reviews and to better understand their target market.

There are several ways NLP can be used for Sentiment Analysis task. These are:

  • Rule-based — uses some NLP techniques such as stemming, tokenizations, part-of-speech tagging. Works as follows: define the words for each group(negative/ positive), count the number of each word groups occurring in a sentence. Depending on the count number of each sentiment group finds if sentence is positive or negative. If even returns neutral. But the rule-based approach may reach up to a very complex system.
  • Automatic — this is where ML is implemented. Sentiment Analysis is usually a classification problem. We have labelled data and trying to find a proper sentiments given the data of sentences with reviews/ comments/ feedbacks etc. The key aspect of this approach is a proper. feature extraction and preparing the feature vectors. For this we use Bag-of-words, Word Embeddings, TF-IDF.
  • Hybrid — combination of the above mentioned techniques. Usually results in more accurate results.

Stay tuned for Airline Sentiment Analysis implementation.

Language Translation


Everyone has ever been in need to translate something very quickly. Be it Google Translate or any other similar application. Such applications are based on a technique called Machine Translation. It is defined as a process of automatically converting text form one language to another without losing the meaning. Comparing to the times when translator applications were built on top of the dictionaries which is a rule-based approach to a problem, the current state-of-the-art machine translation approach (NMT) uses the neural network to predict the likelihood of words. The biggest advantage of NMT compared to SMT (Statistical Machine Translation) and Rule-based, is high speed and capability to learn words and reuse then upon requirement.

Auto Correction and Prediction


Today, there are several programs available that may help us avoid spelling and grammar errors in our emails, messages, and other documents by checking the grammar and spelling of the text we enter (Ex. Grammarly). NLP is crucial technology in this applications. These programs include a wide range of functions, including the ability to suggest synonyms, fix grammatical and spelling errors, rephrase words for clarity, and even estimate the implicit tone of a statement from the user. Another NLP function called “auto prediction” proposes automated text prediction for the text we have already started to type. This helps the user save time and makes their job simpler. These are the steps to building such models:

  • Identify misspelled word.
  • Search for strings that are N-edit-distance from the incorrect word.
  • Filterring.
  • Order based on word probabilities.
  • Choose the best case. Text Prediction is using n-grams based on the likelihood that it’s occurence. Continuous groups of words , tokes, or other symbols that appear in a document are called N-grams.

Targeted Advertising


You would frequently encounter advertisements for such things and other relevant products on other websites if you ever searched for any product or object in any online store. Online targeted advertising of this kind is referred to as targeted advertising and is carried out with the aid of NLP.

The essential component of targeted advertising is keyword matching. Only those who search for a term that is similar to the one with which the advertising was attached will see the Ads, which are linked to a certain word or phrase. That’s obviously insufficient; other factors, such as the subsequent websites they visited and the webpages they looked interested in, are also taken into account to give them the substantial advertisements of products they may be interested in.

Due to the fact that the advertising are only displayed to clients who are truly interested in the product, which is assessed based on their online behavior, many businesses have benefited greatly from this and have saved a significant amount of money.

Chatbots


I personally think of Chatbots as an integral part of most businesses, government sites, providing online services nowadays. They are literally everywhere. So, what makes an AI powered bot different from Rule-based bot ?

We refer to the majority of common bots as “rule-based” bots. They are made to carefully adhere to the conversational guidelines that their author established. When a user enters a certain command, a rule-based bot will automatically provide a prewritten answer. A conventional bot, however, may struggle to give the user valuable information if those guidelines are not followed. The adaptability that is a crucial component of human dialogues is absent. These are the things that mainly make AI chatbot so special and valuable:

  • Understanding Natural Language.
  • The way of communication is no just a Q/A.
  • Continuous improvement. To train a chatbot to understand natural language, you will need a lot of data. Numerous sources, including social media, forums, and customer service records, can be used to get this information. To accommodate many circumstances, the data has to be diversified and labelled.

As a next step you must pre-process the data collected. Tokenizing the text into smaller bits, cleaning and normalizing the data are the crucial steps to perform.

  • Text normalization — maintaining uniformity in the data, convert text to lowercase, remove punctuation, symbols, etc.
  • Tokenization — breaking the text up into smaller pieces, such as words or phrases.
  • Remove Stop Words — terms like “the,” “is,” and “and” from the text that don’t significantly add any meaning to the dataset.
  • Lemmatization — reduces dimensionality of data.
  • Part-of-speech Tagging — identify the grammatical role of each word in the text, such as a noun, verb, or adjective. Then is it essential to understand which NLP technique suit your business requirements. It can be a Sentiment Analysis, Machine Learning based approach, Keyword matching system, or a Language Model.

ChatGPT and NLP

This blogpost should not be ending without mentioning trending ChatGPT. A state-of-the-art natural language processing (NLP) model developed by OpenAI — ChatGPT is a variation of the well-known GPT-3 (Generative Pertained Transformer 3) model, which was trained on a huge amount of text data (~ 570GB of datasets, including web pages, books, and other sources) to produce human-like replies. It is an expansion of the Large Language Model (LLM) class of machine learning Natural Language Processing models. Huge amounts of textual information are ingested by LLMs, which then infer associations between words in the text. As computer power has improved over the past few years, these models have expanded. LLMs get more powerful as their input datasets and parameter space get bigger. Language models analyze the text in the data to calculate the likelihood of the subsequent word. Several probabilistic methods are used to train language models. The GPT-3 is a family of models rather than a single model. The amount of trainable parameters varies among members of the same family of models. Quite a powerful tool with sufficient amount of limitations for now. Looking forward to investigate and test GPT4…

Sentiment Analysis — Sample Codes

Let’s try to perform a Sentiment Analysis on Airline dataset.

import nltk from nltk.corpus import stopwords from nltk.corpus import wordnet as wn from nltk.stem.wordnet import WordNetLemmatizer

from nltk.tokenize import word_tokenize


Above we can see the main imports Natural Language Toolkit — a collection of libraries for NLP and stopwords — collection of words that don’t provide any meaning to a sentence, thus should be removed from the dataset.

dataset = pd.read_csv("../input/airline/airline_sentiment_analysis.csv")


dataset.info()


Checked that we don’t have any null values in our dataset.

colors=sns.color_palette() pd.Series(dataset['airline_sentiment']).value_counts().plot(kind='pie',colors=colors,labels=['Negative','Postive'],explode=[0.05,0.05],shadow=True,autopct='%.2f',fontsize=12,figsize=(6,6)) plt.show()


It is evident that we have imbalanced data towards the negative tweets. Therefore, we have to use the techniques to handle imbalanced data. SMOTE is one of the techniques that will generate a synthetic data to increase the number of positive cases in a balanced way.

` def cleaning_text(tweet): letters_only = re.sub("[^a-zA-Z]", " ",tweet) no_ats= np.vectorize(remove_pattern)(tweet, "@[\w]*") words = no_ats.lower().split() stops = set(stopwords.words("english")) meaningful_words = [w for w in words if not w in stops]

return( " ".join( meaningful_words ))

dataset['clean_text']=dataset['text'].apply(lambda x: cleaning_text(x))`
Lemmatizing — eliminates all suffices and leaves only the root of the word.

`
lemmatizer = WordNetLemmatizer()

def word_lemmatizer(text): word_list = nltk.word_tokenize(text) lem_text = ' '.join([lemmatizer.lemmatize(w) for w in word_list]) return lem_text`

The above code snippet is just a quick and simple cleaning of the text which removes the stop words, numbers and special characters from the text column.

def convert_Sentiment(sentiment): if sentiment == "positive": return 1 elif sentiment == "negative": return 0

The airline sentiment column was originally a String type column so we have to convert and make it numeric.


Now we have to do the same for the cleaned text dataset. We are going to use TF-IDF vectorizer which converts the words into vectors by considering not only the number of times when a word appears but also the importance of that word.

tfid = TfidfVectorizer()

X_vect = tfid.fit_transform(dataset['clean_tweet'])

Now getting back to imbalanced data problem as we left it pending.

smote = SMOTE() x_sm,y_sm = smote.fit_resample(X_vect,y)


Now that we are done with the dataset preparation we can reach our final steps of training the model and testing the results.

` rf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=200) fit = rf.fit(X_train,y_train)

pred = fit.predict(X_test)

accuracy = accuracy_score(pred,y_test)

print('Accuracy:'+str(accuracy)) print(classification_report(pred,y_test)) cm=confusion_matrix(pred , y_test)

`


Monitoring the customer feedbacks and getting the sentiments from them helps the businesses to improve the quality of products and services. Thus, resulting in higher sales. There would be a much more value of this model is we could get data of a longer timeframe.
